content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 56: The narrative checkpoint

The free 14 days got the reps moving. The paid track is where the habit has to become professionally useful. Today belongs to communication and meetings: one practical workout, one usable output, one more piece of your AI working system.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
---------------
ANALYSIS & SYNTHESIS | PUSH | 20 min

THE BRIEF:
Check whether your communication has a story, not just information.

THE SITUATION:
Bring an update, note, or presentation spine. Test the narrative.

PROMPT SCAFFOLD:
Do not copy this blindly. Adapt it to your work.

"I am working on [real situation]. My goal is [specific outcome]. The audience or user is [person/team/customer/reader]. Here is the source material or context I am allowed to share: [paste safe material]. The constraints are [length, tone, deadline, format, privacy, things to avoid]. Help me with: Check whether your communication has a story, not just information. Before producing the final output, tell me what assumptions you are making and what information would improve the result."

YOUR REPS:
1. Ask the model to identify the before, tension, turning point, evidence, and after.

2. Ask where the story is weak or missing.

3. Rewrite one section to make the change clearer.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
- The communication has movement from one state to another.
- Evidence supports the turning point.
- The rewrite improves meaning, not just drama.

TRANSFER:
This transfers to agendas, meeting notes, presentations, stakeholder updates, difficult messages, FAQs, and the general fight against communication that looks finished but does not change anything.

COACHING NOTE:
Humans remember change better than inventory. Narrative is how information becomes usable.

BEHAVIOURAL DESIGN:
Add a one-line "what changed?" to your update. This is your implementation intention for today: after you finish the workout, decide exactly where this output will live and when you will use it again.

MINIMUM VIABLE REP:
If today is chaos, do the smallest honest version: spend five minutes creating the input and five minutes reviewing the output. Do not aim for a masterpiece. Aim for a usable artefact and one clear improvement. Keeping the chain alive matters because skill comes from repeated contact with real work, not from admiring the concept from a safe distance.

---------------
Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive. Treat AI outputs as drafts, not authority. Verify factual claims before using them in public, financial, medical, legal, HR, or high-stakes contexts.
The AI Habit: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
review_notes: Generated as part of the 90-Day AI Habit paid founding track.
Continues from the free 14-day starter sequence without duplicating the foundation.
Organised by professional capability themes: creation, research, decisions, creativity, management, communication, productivity, analysis, workflow, judgement, and workplace adoption.
Draws from official prompt guidance and behavioural science principles without copying source programme content.
